Barrel Sauna UK

Floating Sauna Proposal  ·  Prepared for Sanju
01 · Introduction

A floating sauna, built for the long game

You came to us with a clear idea: a commercial floating sauna on the water, built right the first time, the first step in the nationwide business you are planning.

This proposal introduces the concept we are picturing for your pilot, shows you the work we already have running as live commercial businesses, and sets out an estimated cost at this early stage. It also explains exactly what the design commitment secures, and what happens next.

What follows is an initial concept and an estimate. The bespoke design, drawn around your site and your guests, comes once you are on board.

Having everything right on the first go is non-negotiable for us.

Your words on the call, and the principle this whole proposal is built around.

02 · About Barrel Sauna UK

A Welsh family business, handmade to order

We are a family-run manufacturer based in Wales, building bespoke saunas and wellness structures from scratch for homes and commercial venues across the UK and Ireland.

Every unit we make is built to order in our own workshop. Nothing is flat-packed, nothing is imported and rebadged. We design and fabricate the whole thing ourselves, which is what lets us tailor each build to the client rather than the other way round.

That matters most on a commercial project. When a build has to run as a live business, meet the relevant compliance requirements, and stand up to daily use, being the people who actually made it is the difference between a supplier and a partner. We design around those requirements and work with the appropriate specialists where needed. We are UK-based, we answer the phone, and when something needs attention we make the trip.

03 · Why clients choose us

Made in Wales, and built to last

The reasons clients pick us over a cheaper import are the same reasons a well-built commercial unit lasts, where a cheaper flat-pack often would not.

Manufactured in WalesDesigned and fabricated in our own workshop, start to finish. We control the build, so we can tailor it to you.
Bespoke, built to orderNo two builds are the same. Layout, glazing, heating and finish are all specified around your site and your guests.
UK-based aftercareDevon, Cornwall, Scotland, Ireland. Anything within warranty and within reason, we come back out and put it right.
Sustainability

Timber that gives back

Our builds are made from premium thermowood, heat-treated for durability and stability without chemical preservatives. It is a material chosen for long-term durability when correctly maintained, which is the most sustainable thing a structure can do: not need replacing.

We plant ten trees for every one we use, so the material at the heart of every build is renewed many times over rather than simply spent. A commitment made consistently, on every project we take on.

Every sauna supports a greener future
We plant 10 trees for every one we use

05 · Why we can build this

A floating platform is a natural next step for us

A floating sauna is not something we have only just decided to do. We have been planning it for some time, and we see it as the natural progression from our commercial mobile saunas. What carries over is the hard part: fabricating the structure.

We already manufacture our own trailer chassis, steel frames and supporting structures in-house. Designing a floating platform draws on exactly that experience: load, balance, a steel base built to carry a timber structure safely. It is an extension of what we do every week, not a leap into the unknown.

That in-house fabrication is also why we can stand behind a bespoke commercial build. We manufacture the structure ourselves, working with the appropriate marine and certification specialists where required, so when your project needs something specific, we can make it.

06 · Initial concept

How we are thinking about the layout

A glass-fronted, wood-fired straight-edge sauna on a floating pontoon, designed around privacy, compliance, and low-maintenance running.

The concept below carries the essentials you set out on the call: a wood-burning stove with the log feed running through the changing room, a changing room at each end kept private, a covered front deck, and warm, low lighting behind the backrests and under the seats only, so the space stays private for guests in swimwear. A dedicated staff and storage area is also captured here, positioned and developed during the design stage, and the three cold plunge tubs you mentioned are set out below.

The layout shown reflects our initial interpretation of your brief, and will be refined with you during the design stage until it is right for your site.

Alongside the sauna, the three cold plunge tubs you mentioned are designed to work as part of a guided contrast-therapy experience, cycling guests between the heat of the sauna and the cold of the plunge. Running the tubs as a set, rather than a single bath, keeps groups moving through the hot and cold stages without a bottleneck, which is what turns a session into a repeatable booking. Their number, placement and the flow between sauna and plunge are developed during the design stage.

Concept illustration only. The visuals shown are to communicate the initial idea. The final design, layout and specification will be developed during the paid design stage.

07 · Estimated cost

An estimate to plan around

The figures below are indicative, to help you plan, and are not a fixed quotation. The precise specification and final costs are confirmed once the design stage begins.

  • Sauna and changing roomsThe commercial floating sauna and changing rooms, on its pontoon.£90,000 to £120,000 plus VAT
  • Office and storage podA 2.4m by 2.4m pod with folding table and chair, double doors and a rear opening window.from £8,500 plus VAT
  • Three cold plunge tubsA set of three, for guided contrast-therapy sessions.from £18,000 plus VAT
Estimated total
£116,500 to £146,500 plus VAT

The estimated total for the build items above. Delivery and certification are separate, and the final figure is confirmed at the design stage, once the layout, specification and site are locked.

  • Delivery to siteSeparate · varies by mooring location
  • CertificationSeparate · arranged per site

Delivery and certification vary by where the unit is moored and how it is brought to site, so they are kept as separate line items rather than folded into the headline figures. We work these through with you once the design is underway and the logistics are known.

What happens next

From sign-off to delivery

  • Sign-off and orderWhen the design is right and you are ready, your build goes into the queue and the balance terms are confirmed.
  • Build and lead timeManufacturing lead time is around seven to eight weeks. The overall project timeline depends on the pontoon, certification and delivery logistics, so plan for a few months from order to delivery.
  • Install and handoverOur team installs, walks every element with you on site, and signs off, which activates your warranty.

An initial concept, and a starting point

Thank you for considering Barrel Sauna UK for your floating sauna. When you are ready, the design commitment allows us to begin developing your bespoke concept.
